title: festuca ovina subsp. supina
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. supina, also known as sheep's fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Festuca ovina subsp. supina has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Festuca ovina subsp. supina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine for its potential to treat various conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
